summary: Tested new exhibitor Social Post features (photo framing + luxury fonts + /api/uploads path). Backend: 54/55 pytest tests pass (98.2%). All new endpoints/fields validated: photo_focus_x/y/zoom in /auth/me and persisted via PUT /exhibitors/me; POST /api/upload returns /api/uploads/... URL that is publicly reachable; GET /api/exhibitors/me/social-post.png returns a valid 3000x3000 PNG (>50KB, 'magic bytes' verified); framing values affect PNG bytes (hash differs). One legacy expectation fails: /uploads/<file> path is NOT publicly reachable through ingress (returns SPA HTML) — only /api/uploads/* is routed to backend by k8s ingress. This is an ingress routing limitation, not a backend code bug — backend's legacy mount exists but is unreachable through the public URL. Frontend: exhibitor login -> dashboard works; all required data-testid attributes present (exhibitor-dashboard, ex-jump-social, photo-adjuster, photo-frame-preview, photo-frame-zoom, photo-frame-reset, photo-frame-save, social-download, social-share, social-post-card); oval preview correctly displays profile photo and is responsive to drag + zoom slider; Apply Framing button triggers PUT to backend (button changes to 'Saving...').

minor.issue: Legacy /uploads/* path is unreachable through public ingress (returns SPA HTML 200). Only the new /api/uploads/* path is routed to backend. The legacy fallback claim in the problem statement is not true via the public URL. Acceptable since DB has been migrated to /api/uploads/ URLs.

action_items: (Optional) If older clients/links still point to /uploads/<file> we should add an ingress rule for it or proactively migrate any DB rows that still store /uploads/ to /api/uploads/. Currently the seeded exhibitor profile_photo_url is already /api/uploads/ so functionally everything works.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~1071 lines — exceeds 700-line guideline. Consider splitting into routers/<resource>.py modules (auth, exhibitors, upload, social_post).

Social post rendering is synchronous in the request handler. A 3000x3000 PIL render with image paste + multiple TTF lookups can take 2-5s; under load, consider caching the generated PNG keyed on (exhibitor_id, photo_focus_x, photo_focus_y, photo_zoom, business_name, photo_url, updated_at).

Photo framing values from PUT /exhibitors/me are not validated against documented ranges (0..1 for focus, 1..3 for zoom). Pydantic Field constraints (ge=0.0, le=1.0 for focus; ge=1.0, le=3.0 for zoom) should be added to prevent edge-case PIL crops outside the photo bounds.
_id is correctly excluded in exhibitor responses — good.

Frontend ExhibitorDashboard.jsx 'Building your post...' shows while postImageUrl is null but the social-download/share buttons stay disabled with no spinner-tied loading indicator on those buttons themselves. Minor UX polish opportunity.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all data-testid present, photo framing flow works, PUT triggered, oval shows face correctly)

test_credentials: Admin: admin@admin.com / Admin@123 ; Seeded Exhibitor: mobile 9876543210 / Exhibit@123 (profile_photo_url=/api/uploads/4b69883cc717425f9654bc077769332d.jpg)
seed_data_creation: None — used pre-seeded exhibitor account. PUT mutations to focus/zoom persist; if a fresh state is needed, call PUT /api/exhibitors/me with {photo_focus_x:0.5, photo_focus_y:0.35, photo_zoom:1.0}.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_rama_bazaar.py now has TestSocialPost class with 6 tests covering /auth/me framing fields, PUT persistence, 3000x3000 PNG validation, hash diff between framings, and profile photo URL resolution. TestUpload has been updated to assert /api/uploads/ prefix. The single failing test (test_legacy_uploads_path_still_works) documents a known ingress limitation; main agent may decide to xfail/remove it or add ingress rule. UI: photo framing flow confirmed working visually — face is positioned correctly inside the oval (see /app/.screenshots/05_after_apply.png).
rca of the issue: Legacy /uploads/* path failure: Kubernetes ingress only forwards /api/* to the backend service (port 8001); any other path falls through to the frontend SPA's catch-all that returns index.html. Backend has app.mount('/uploads', ...) for back-compat but that mount is never reached through the public URL. Mitigation: rely exclusively on /api/uploads/* (already done in current code) and/or add ingress rule for /uploads/*.
